content:
- Read current task and executed plan (NOW EXECUTE) for splitting ultra-long HA no-quorum e2e into shorter parallel-safe tests.
- Verified gates; initially hit `make test` 120s timeout and a flaky no-quorum HA-state poll (response missing status line).
- Implemented: held port reservations with JIT release; shortened/robust HA polling for no-quorum; reduced workload SQL timeouts; improved postgres shutdown fallback by killing postmaster PID when pg_ctl times out; refactored no-quorum fencing test to use quorum-loss cutoff (keeps runtime under 120s).
- Added mapping doc under docs and updated SUMMARY; Makefile enforces 120s `make test` timeout and rejects RUST_TEST_THREADS=1; `make test-long` has no timeout and currently no ultra-long tests.
- Final verification: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test` (cargo test ~93.82s under 120s wrapper), `make test-long` all green.
- Marked task md completed/passing, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed + pushed.
